title: Horrific Regret over all my breast cancer treatment. Should NEVER have gotten biopsy and NEVER done lumpectomy. So angry and upset.
body:
TRIGGER WARNING:  This is a post that discusses a different perspective on BC that is not "I am so glad I got rid of cancer from my body at any cost" - this post comes from the perspective of, "There are more risks to current SOC treatment than benefits for some women who have other values/priorities"

​

I wish there had been others like me posting a few weeks ago when I was in the throes of the Breast Cancer Fear Factory.  Dear other person who is like me, concerned MORE about living with the aesthetic and functional (pain free, movement at full range of motion) outcome of your body than "removing cancer at all costs".  Both perspectives are valid.  Both should be talked about.  If you don't want a scar, if you want your boobs to remain the same, that is valid and should be honored.  Plastics CANNOT make your boobs match perfectly ever again.  Your life with your great tits is OVER if you choose surgery.  Done.  Don't buy into these fear appeals about immediate action.  Standard of Care is invasive care; but there are minimally invasive methods (cryo, radiofrequency, microwave ablation) that exist.  Everyone deserves to live their lives how they want.  If you want to live with your boobs looking great and possibly (but not probably) reduce your duration of life, that is your choice and absolutely good for you.  I support you!  It's not unreasonable - there isn't a lot of research out there, but what exists does show suicide rates are increased for those who get mastectomies, especially if they had less invasive cancers.   Sidenote: double check everything - pathology, images, everything should be sent to a second lab unaffiliated with your first location to have everything double checked.

I had PERFECT gorgeous boobs that are now lumpy and mismatched.  There was no need to maim them with lumpectomy.  I can only hope my one-shot one-time radiation which is supposed to have less aesthetic and health side effects doesn't do more damage.  

1.  I regret being pushed into my biopsy.  I said no to a biopsy 2 times.  I told them I did not want a biopsy, thank you very much.  It was like a car salesman and I was pushed into it.  I cried the entire time and told them I did not want this. 
2. When I asked what they suspected it was, they wouldn't tell me.  They wouldn't give me the BiRads, even when I asked and proposed the BiRads scale.  They should inform women, "we think this suspicious spot is X cancer" (ie: LCIS, DCIS, IDC - they \*do\* know likelihood because there \*are\* patterns in mammo/US/MRI to these things), before you have a biopsy.  Having this info would have sent me right to the journals to read the likelihood of Death By Cancer for the suspected type and how many years until DBC before agreeing to a biopsy.  My mammo showed classic signs of the type that my cells were. 
3. They should give you a choice of biopsy type.  Fine needle or cryo biopsy would be a great option, if I had been able to choose.  The core needle biopsy caused my boob to get inflamed and itchy, it irritated a lymph node, which caused me insane amounts of trauma and distress, as I imagined this was metastasizing.  
4. They should give you ample time to read the consent form.  The form doesn't change - email that to the patient a week beforehand.  Do not give it to her while she is laying down and trying to read while getting swabbed. 

Those are just my biopsy regrets.  Now I am 1.5 weeks out from lumpectomy and I can now live crying and regretting my lumpectomy.  The only surgery in my life I did not want to have.  

Fear is used to motivate women to comply with large data health research throughout the breast cancer machine - from trying to get us to do yearly mammograms to biopsy to surgical choices to trying to get us to accept radiation which is shown in journals to reduce quality of life as well as duration of life.  They use fear to get compliance, which is a health communication tactic ("fear appeals").   When in reality, they don't know who or which cancer gets "bad".  For most cancers, they can't tell.  They can't predict this reliably yet, so they overtreat everyone in hopes of correctly treating one person.  It's OK to say NO to being treated with Standard of Care, which is based on 15-20yo data.

The first surgeon I was assigned told me I would get worse in a year if I didn't listen to her.  Data shows 98% of the women with my type of cells would be alive in 5 years and a little less than that would be alive in 10 by doing NOTHING.  Fearmongering is bad medicine.

I 100% regret my lumpectomy.  I wanted to run out of there the day of the surgery, but I rationalized it by saying that they would cut out one small piece and I would be fine.  Well, radiology did a bad job on mammo image locations, MRI radiologist probably was lazy and used mammo location, so surgeon dug out 3 fibroadenomas before he found the correct location.  Thanks a fuckton.  I didn't consent to anything extra being removed but the identified malignant cells, but now I have a whole bunch of boob missing that could have lived in there without problem.  I am working on getting my records because I am going to make sure that the MRI and Mammo radiologist knows they did a fuckup that has now greatly impacted my mental health and life moving forward.  They never get that loop closed with what happens after they turn in their reports and I imagine they go home and feel they are doing God's work for women with breast cancer.  Nope, you are subjecting some women to a lifetime of pain, aesthetic change, and absolute mental devastation.

So, if you are out there upset, just diagnosed, thinking you HAVE to accept a lumpectomy or MX, guess what - you can SAY NO.  You can think about it.  You can think for years and months.  You can get regular US and monitor it weekly.  You can say NO and get cryoablation which is just as good.  YOU DO NOT HAVE TO BE PRESSURED BY FAMILY, FRIENDS, and the MEDICAL COMMUNITY TO RUIN YOUR BOOB IF YOU LIKE YOUR BOOB!  YOU CAN SAY NO TO ANY TREATMENT OFFERED.  You are going to have to say No multiple times.  They might berate you, like the radiologist and surgeon did to me, but you can SAY NO.  This is not a "journey" - that's bullshit terminology.  This is traumatic experience that is not managed well and where full side effects are not communicated even when you ask directly. All I feel right now is "I was victimized by the breast cancer machine".  

We don't often hear from all the women happily living their lives who just did monitoring (that study where women just watched/monitored is currently wrapping up and surprise!  there are LOADS of women who monitored their sticky spot for 5 years with no issues).  

Dear Similar Me who might have found this post because they were looking to see if people regret cancer surgery:  YES.  PEOPLE DO REGRET HAVING SURGERY.  I regret lumpectomy.  I regret it wholeheartedly and I picked a fantastic surgeon who is oncoplastic trained. I am angry I was made fearful and am now unhappy with my appearance, just as I expected was a possible result.  The breast cancer medical complex loves to say, "plastic surgery can fix it!"  - PLASTIC CANNOT GIVE YOU YOUR MATCHING BOOBS BACK.  They Cannot.  And if you do radiation, that's highly unlikely.  

To the person who might be seeking all this out (because everything is so elementary education fluff), know thyself.  If your boobs are important to you it is valid for you to want to keep them and it is valid for you to prioritize keeping them As-Is.  For many types of cancers (DCIS, LCIS), it is 75-80% chance your cancer cells will never migrate elsewhere.   Do not do any surgery you don't want the outcome to.    I was freaked out by the constant fear messaging; my armpit started to hurt (from the biopsy inflammation impacting my node) but that caused even more fear from the providers, and I made decisions that were contrary to what I know I wanted for myself. 

I wouldn't be up at 2am writing this if I was OK and Happy and feeling like a Survivor.  I feel tricked, pissed off, and wish to high hell there was a post like this I could have found and read in January.  Guess what, I would be sleeping and still Surviving if I hadn't done jack shit, including not doing the biopsy or mammogram.  And data shows I'd be just fine in 10 years + as well.  

​

There's more, but obvs this is a rant, so I'll do bullet points another time. 

PS: Turning down all tamoxifen/AIs, because they do more harm than good.  Reduces by 40% your personal odds, so 8% recurrence becomes 5% but you increase your uterine cancer risk, DVT, PE, stroke, and dementia odds - No Thank You.  Plus, the studies the hormone blockers were based on the estimate is about 60% of participants didn't take the drugs in a recommended manner.  

​
